title: cicuta occidentalis f. frondosa
intro: Cicuta occidentalis f. frondosa, also known as Western Water Hemlock, is a highly toxic and poisonous plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, the roots of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, although it is crucial to exercise extreme caution due to its deadly toxicity, with medicinal properties including emetic, purgative, and anthelmintic properties. In small and controlled amounts, it may have health benefits such as treating parasitic infections and purging the body of toxins, however, its use is not recommended for the general public. Herbal preparations of Western Water Hemlock typically involve dried root extracts and should only be prepared by experienced herbalists, who use it in minimal amounts for specific and monitored medical purposes.
